title: parse: add support for relabel bind mount option
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind bug

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

Fixes #4513

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
add support for `relabel=[shared|private]` bind mount option
```
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]
Signed-off-by: danishprakash <danish.prakash@suse.com>

comments.body:
@flouthoc Sure, I'm trying to figure out what the best way to do that would be. I'm assuming we just don't want to test whether we _can_ apply the `relabel` mount option but the actual functionality. I don't have an SELinux system available atm which I'm guessing would be required to write and assert the tests. Or am I missing something here?

In theory, I could simply label a file on host, mount it during build with the `relabel` option, and then check `ls -Z` on the file for confirmation, wdyt?
